title: Understanding the OWASP Top 10
short_description: A deep dive into the most critical security risks to web applications.
content:
The OWASP Top 10 is a standard awareness document for developers and web application security. It represents a broad consensus about the most critical security risks to web applications. Here are the top three:

  1. Broken Access Control: Restrictions on what authenticated users are allowed to do are often not properly enforced.
  2. Cryptographic Failures: Sensitive data such as passwords, health records, and personal info require extra protection.
  3. Injection: Cross-site scripting and SQL injection are frequent and high-impact risks.

By understanding these threats, developers can build more secure applications from the ground up.

title: The Basics of Symmetric Encryption
short_description: How Fernet and AES protect your data at rest and in transit.
content:
Symmetric encryption is a type of cryptographic algorithm that uses the same key to encrypt and decrypt data. Fernet, a popular standard built around AES, is employed in many modern applications to safely store secrets.

How does it work?

  • A key is generated securely.
  • The plaintext is transformed into ciphertext using the key.
  • The ciphertext can only be reversed back to plaintext by an entity possessing the exact same key.

Remember: If the key is leaked, all your encrypted data is exposed!

title: Why Passwords Must Be Hashed
short_description: Hash algorithms like SHA-256 vs Plaintext.
content:
Storing passwords in plaintext is one of the most cardinal sins in cybersecurity. If a database is breached, the attacker will have immediate access to all user accounts.

Instead, passwords must be hashed. A good hashing algorithm has these properties:

  • Deterministic: The same password always gives the same hash.
  • Irreversible: It's practically impossible to get the password from the hash.
  • Collision Resistant: It's incredibly unlikely that two different passwords produce the same hash.

We typically use algorithms like bcrypt or argon2 which also add salt and compute time to slow down brute force attacks.
